# Patti Smith Real Estate, Garden Valley, California

> Georgetown Divide real estate broker serving Garden Valley and the surrounding El Dorado County foothills. Specialist in rural and agricultural property: acreage, fertile farmland, wells, septic, water rights, fire hazard zones, equestrian property, and Gold Country homes.

## About
- Broker: Patti Smith (Patti Smith Real Estate, Independent)
- License: CA DRE #01110483
- Office: 6180 State Highway 193, Georgetown, CA 95634
- Phone: (530) 368-0852
- Serving the Sierra Nevada foothills since 1992
- Georgetown Divide resident; past president of the Georgetown Rotary Club; 12-year director of the Georgetown Fire Department

## Garden Valley, CA at a glance
- Unincorporated community in El Dorado County, formerly named Johntown, on Johntown Creek; elevation about 1,949 feet, lower and warmer than Georgetown
- Population roughly 2,650; median age about 45; ZIP 95633
- Fertile valley settled in the Gold Rush, when miners farmed its soil to feed the mining camps; James Marshall was a regular visitor
- Home of the historic Black Oak Mine, namesake of the Black Oak Mine Unified School District
- Largely within high fire hazard severity zones; naturally occurring asbestos in foothill soils is a regional development consideration
- Water: Georgetown Divide Public Utility District and El Dorado Irrigation District, plus many private wells and septic systems
- Schools: Black Oak Mine Unified School District; Golden Sierra Junior Senior High (grades 7-12) is located in Garden Valley at 5101 Garden Valley Road
- Typical market: move-ready homes on acreage roughly $400,000 to $700,000; Divide-area median near $439,000; raw and agricultural land from about $50,000; fenced agricultural estates $1,000,000 to $2,000,000
